answer:
**In Manusmṛti chapter 6, the vanaprastha (forest-dweller or hermit) stage of life is described starting at verse 6.1, where a householder who has seen signs of old age retreats to the forest.**[^1]

**The diet consists of wild, uncultivated "forest produce" gathered personally—vegetables, roots, fruits, and flowers from pure (uncultivated) trees, along with limited other items like self-prepared salt or oils from fruits.**[^2] **Key passages specify:**

> With various pure foods of hermits, or with vegetables, roots, and fruits, he should perform these same great sacrifices according to the rule.[^3]

> With pure hermit-foods gathered by himself in spring and autumn, he should duly prepare sacrificial cakes and boiled rice-offerings separately. Having offered that very pure forest oblation to the deities, he should use the remainder for himself, and also salt prepared by himself.[^4]

> He may eat vegetables that grow on land and in water, and flowers, roots, and fruits that come from pure trees, and oils produced from fruits. He should avoid honey and meat, and mushrooms growing from the earth, and the Bhustrina and Shigruka plants, and the fruits of the Shleshmataka tree.[^5]

> He should not eat what is grown on ploughed land, even if it has been thrown away by someone; nor roots and fruits grown in a village, even if he is in distress.[^6]

> Or he may live always on flowers, roots, and fruits alone, which have ripened in time and fallen of their own accord, abiding in the rule of the Vaikhanasas.[^7]

**... living on roots and fruits.[^8]**

**Prohibitions emphasize avoiding cultivated or village produce to maintain purity, and periodic discarding of gathered food (e.g., in Āśvayuja).**[^9]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, ch. 6 (full chapter). [/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/06/translation/]
[^2]: Ibid., verses 6.1–6.33 describe the *vanaprastha* rules.
[^3]: Ibid., verse 6.5 ([p-?] from body; part of early vanaprastha instructions).
[^4]: Ibid., verses 6.11–6.12.
[^5]: Ibid., verses 6.13–6.14.
[^6]: Ibid., verse 6.16.
[^7]: Ibid., verse 6.21.
[^8]: Ibid., verse 6.25.
[^9]: Ibid., verse 6.15.
